name: nonzero_has_set_bit
reason: Any nonzero u64 has a set bit below 64 — this is mathematically correct and expected, serving as a sanity check that the lemma is usable at the top level

name: hi_1_forces_bit_0
reason: With hi=1 the only possible index is 0 and a must be 1 — tests that the recursion base case is tight

name: result_always_less_than_64
code:
proof fn phi_3_result_always_less_than_64(a: u64, hi: u64) -> (i: u64)
    requires
        a != 0,
        hi <= 64,
        a >> hi == 0,
    ensures
        i < 64,
{
    lemma_obtain_bit_index_1_aux(a, hi)
}
reason: Since `i < hi` and `hi <= 64`, the result is always below 64 — tests that the bound propagates correctly through the ensures

name: bit_set_implies_nonzero_mask
reason: `is_bit_set(a, i)` should imply `a & (1 << i) != 0` — tests that the ensures is strong enough to derive nonzero mask
